title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Program Brief: Text Alternative
summary: Walls, visited cells, current focus, route, and errors are communicated only by color and animation, while the Canvas has no usable alternative. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the text alternative increment in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Demonstrate pure-core tests, fake adapters, dependency inspection, native adapter smoke, and changed implementation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-text-alternative — Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_accessible_interface_4(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"
